'''Jason Clark''' (born 28 June 1989) is an Australian former [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|lf}} for the [[Limoux Grizzlies]] in the [[Elite One Championship]].

He played for the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL), the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]] and played for [[City New South Wales rugby league team|NSW City]] in 2015.

==Background==
Clark was born in [[Sydney]], New South Wales, Australia.{{cn|date=July 2025}}

==Playing career==
===Early career===
Clark played his junior football for the [[Coogee Randwick Wombats]], representing New South Wales at under-17 level.<ref name="South Sydney Profile">{{cite web|url=http://www.rabbitohs.com.au/playerprofiledisplay/Rabbitohs/Jason%20Clark/4003|title=Player Profile Display|website=Rabbitohs.com.au|date=2013-06-09|access-date=2013-06-09|archive-date=13 July 2013|archive-url=https://archive.today/20130713025528/http://www.rabbitohs.com.au/playerprofiledisplay/Rabbitohs/Jason%20Clark/4003|url-status=dead}}</ref> Prior to his NRL debut, he was Souths' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|Toyota Cup]] [[Captain (sports)|captain]], scoring 20 tries in 47 NYC games for the South Sydney side.<ref name="rleague.com Profile">{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com/db/content/84000/84988.php |title=rleague.com Profile |website=Rleague.com |date=2009-12-14 |access-date=2009-12-14 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100103082424/http://rleague.com/db/content/84000/84988.php |archive-date= 3 January 2010 }}</ref>

===2008===
Clark played for South Sydney's [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team as captain. He won the first Rabbitohs NYC Players Player award.

===2009===
In round 25 of the [[2009 NRL season]], Clark made his first grade debut for Souths (first grade player number 1072)<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.ssralmanac.com/mobile/players_c/player_clark_jason.htm|title = South Sydney Rabbitohs Rugby League Player Report - Jason Clark}}</ref> in a 41-6 win over [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] where he was a [[Rugby league positions#Substitutes|replacement]], coming off of the bench at [[Rugby league positions#Substitutes|hooker]].

===2010===
Clark played in 20 matches for Souths in the [[2010 NRL season]] playing three games at lock, one game in the second row, and the rest from the bench.

===2011===
In round 18, Clark scored his first NRL try in Souths 36-22 loss against Manly. Clark played in 15 matches for Souths in the [[2011 NRL season]].

===2012===
[[File:Jason Clark South Sydney v Warrington 2012.jpg|thumb|right|Clark playing for the Rabbitohs in 2012]]
In 2012, Clark went to play a total of 23 out of 25 matches for Souths and scored one try. This was Clark's first year of making it into the finals, however Souths were knocked out of the finals in the preliminary final against [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs|Canterbury-Bankstown]].

===2013===
On 9 February 2013, Clark captained Souths in the 2013 "Return to Redfern" clash against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rabbitohs.com.au/news/2013/02/04/clark-to-captain-rabbitohs-at-return-to-redfern.html|title=Clark To Captain Rabbitohs at Return to Redfern|publisher=Rabbitohs|access-date=2016-09-17}}</ref> His team won 38-12, with Clark scoring a double. 
[[File:Jason Clark.jpg|thumb|right|Clark playing for the South Sydney Rabbitohs in 2013]]
On 22 September 2013, Clark was named on the interchange bench in the 2013 [[New South Wales Cup]] Team of the Year.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nswrl.com.au/default.aspx?s%3Darticle-display%26id%3D84880%26title%3D2013-nswrlcomau-vb-nsw-cup-team-of-the-year---announcement |title=2013 NSWRL.com.au VB NSW Cup Team of the Year - Announcement - New South Wales Rugby League |access-date=2016-02-04 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140111155627/http://www.nswrl.com.au/default.aspx?s=article-display&id=84880&title=2013-nswrlcomau-vb-nsw-cup-team-of-the-year---announcement |archive-date=11 January 2014 }}</ref>  Clark only played 11 games for Souths in [[2013 NRL season]].

===2014===
Clark missed the first four weeks of the [[2014 NRL season]] with an ankle injury.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-preseason-casualty-ward-injuries-and-surgeries-at-your-club-ahead-of-2014/story-fn2mcuj6-1226755029248|title=Home | Live Scores & Latest News|work=[[Fox Sports]]|access-date=2016-09-17}}</ref> He scored a try on his return in round five, where Souths beat the Dragons 26-6, he then played all games excluding one in the lead up to Souths 21st Premiership. On 5 October 2014, in South Sydney's [[2014 NRL Grand Final]] against the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]], Clark played off the bench in Souths 30-6 victory.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/south-sydney-rabbitohs-win-2014-nrl-grand-final-defeating-canterbury-bulldogs-306/story-fni3fbgz-1227080755667|title=South Sydney Rabbitohs Win 2014 NRL Grand Final Defeating Canterbury Bulldogs 30-6|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2016-09-17}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/rabbitohs-grand-final-player-ratings/tabid/10874/newsid/82375/default.aspx|title=Rabbitohs Grand Final Player Ratings|website=NRL.com|date=5 October 2014|access-date=2016-09-17}}</ref>

===2015===
Clark was named to play in South Sydney's [[2015 NRL Auckland Nines]] squad in jersey 11.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rabbitohs.com.au/news/2015/01/23/rabbitohs_squad_for_.html|title=Squad for 2015 Dick Smith NRL Auckland Nines Tournament Next Weekend|date=23 January 2015|publisher=Rabbitohs|access-date=2016-09-17}}</ref> Clark played in all of the games and scored Souths first try of the tournament; against the [[Penrith Panthers]] where he was awarded a 5-point try for scoring right under the posts.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rabbitohs.com.au/news/2015/01/31/rabbits_open_nines_c.html|title=Rabbits Open Nines Campaign with Victory|date=31 January 2015|publisher=Rabbitohs|access-date=2016-09-17}}</ref> Clark played in the [[2015 World Club Series|2015 World Club Challenge]] where Souths beat [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] 39-0. He was originally named to start at lock, however [[Ben Lowe]] started at lock and Clark moved onto the bench. 
[[File:Jason Clark City.jpg|thumb|right|Clark playing for City in 2015]]
On 3 May 2015, Clark played for [[City New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales City]] against [[Country New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales Country]] in the 2015 [[City vs Country Origin]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/representative-round-team-lists/tabid/10874/newsid/85557/default.aspx|title=Representative Round team lists|publisher=NRL.com|date=2015-04-28|access-date=2015-05-03}}</ref>

On 22 May 2015, Clark played his 100th NRL first-grade game for Souths against the [[Parramatta Eels]]. On 12 June 2015, Clark played his 150th grade game for South Sydney. This includes all of his NYC games and first grade games.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rabbitohs.com.au/news/2015/06/11/clark_to_reach_150_g.html|title=Clark to Reach 150 Grade Game Milestone|date=11 June 2015|publisher=Rabbitohs|access-date=2016-09-17}}</ref> Clark went to play 18 NRL games in 2015 for Souths, scoring two tries. Clark was awarded the Burrow Appreciation Award for 2015 at South Sydney's annual Red and Green Ball, an award awarded to a South Sydney player every year nominated by "the burrow", a Rabbitohs supporter group.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rabbitohs.com.au/news/2015/10/09/jason_clark_collects.html|title=Jason Clark Collects Burrow Appreciation Award for 2015|date=9 October 2015|publisher=Rabbitohs|access-date=2016-09-17}}</ref>

===2016===
Clark started the year representing South Sydney in their [[2016 Auckland Nines]] team, playing all three of Souths' games.

On 18 August, Clark extended his contract with Souths until the end of the 2018 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rabbitohs.com.au/news/2016/08/18/rabbitohs_extend_con.html|title=extend contract of Jason Clark until the end of the 2018 season|date=18 August 2016|publisher=Rabbitohs|access-date=2016-09-17}}</ref>

At the conclusion of the [[2016 NRL season]], Clark along with [[Sam Burgess]] and [[Kyle Turner (rugby league)|Kyle Turner]], played in 23 of Souths' 24 matches - the most matches by any player. Despite not scoring any tries in 2016, Clark finished 4th in the team in tackles (with 603) and was also second to last in missed tackles (by players who had played over 10 matches), with only 17.

===2017===
Clark made 15 appearances for Souths in the [[2017 NRL season]]. 
[[File:JASON CLARK.jpg|thumb|right|Clark playing for the North Sydney Bears in 2017]]
Later in the year he played four games for the [[North Sydney Bears]] but was recalled to The Souths team for the final game of the year against Parramatta.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://leagueunlimited.com/news/30925-eels-v-rabbitohs-preview/|title=Eels v Rabbitohs preview - 2017 Round 26 » League Unlimited|website=League Unlimited|access-date=15 June 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTItMTY5Ni00LS0tLS0tMTQwMjYtLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t|title=Custom Match List - Rugby League Project|first=Shawn Dollin and Andrew|last=Ferguson|website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org|access-date=15 June 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://websites.sportstg.com/team_info.cgi?action=PSTATS&pID=194435783&client=0-8908-125894-443396-25897821|title=Jason Clark - Player Statistics|website=SportsTG|access-date=15 June 2018}}</ref>

===2018===
At the start of the [[2018 NRL season]], it was revealed that Souths management had told Clark his services would not be required for the [[2019 NRL season]] and beyond.{{cn|date=July 2023}}

This prompted Souths fans to start a petition demanding that Clark would be offered a new contract to stay at the club.  The petition read “Jason was told by club officials that he is no longer in their plans beyond the 2018 NRL season. Jason plays every game with pure Rabbitohs heart and he's both fan and players favourite. We don't want to see this amazing player leave this club, he played a big part in our 2014 premiership winning series. We absolutely CANNOT see this great talent leave. Please sign and share this petition and hopefully get this message to club officials"<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/private-ryan-petition-to-keep-souths-fan-favourite-nrl-debutant-traditions-revealed/news-story/11cdee813af483d07b75b27dcd7f20b8|title=NRL Rabbitohs Jason Clark, Braidon Burns NRL contract, Cooper Cronk Roosters favourite — Private Ryan|date=22 April 2018|access-date=15 June 2018}}</ref>

In June 2018, Clark signed a two-year deal to join English side Warrington.  Clark was part of the Souths side that ended up finishing 3rd on the table at the end of the regular season.  Clark's final game for Souths was the 13-12 elimination final victory over St George as the player picked up an injury in the match and was ruled out of the preliminary final game against the [[Sydney Roosters]].<ref>{{Cite news | url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/44368409 | title=Warrington sign Souths forward Clark | work=BBC Sport }}</ref><ref>{{cite web| url = https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/departing-clark-out-of-souths-grand-final-qualifier-20180921-p5057v.html| title = Jason Clark ruled out of Souths' NRL grand final qualifier against the Roosters| date = 21 September 2018}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/live-nrl-preliminary-final-south-sydney-rabbitohs-vs-sydney-roosters/news-story/1b96ecdc9c34afaea0b364dbc51d7ca0|title = Cooper Cronk horror after 'cheap shot'|newspaper = News.com.au|date = 22 September 2018|last1 = Blair|first1 = Alex}}</ref>

===2019===
[[File:Jason Clark Warrington Wolves warm up.jpg|thumb|right|Clark warming up for the Warrington Wolves in 2019]]
He played in the [[2019 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-vs-warrington-live-18996678|title=St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley|publisher=Mirror|date=24 August 2019|access-date=1 November 2019}}</ref>

===2023===
On 6 Jun 2023 he announced his retirement from professional rugby league.<ref>{{cite web|website=Warrington Guardian|date=6 Jun 2023|accessdate=8 Oct 2023|title=Jason Clark; Ex-South Sydney and Warrington man retires|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/wolves/wolvesnews/23569872.jason-clark-ex-south-sydney-warrington-man-retires/}}</ref>
==Personal life==
Clark is an Australian Apprenticeships Ambassador for the Australian Government and an Apprentice Mentor in the NRL's Trade UP With The NRL Program.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.australianapprenticeships.gov.au/ambassador/jason-clark|title=Carpentry 'a natural fit': Jason Clark|publisher=Australian Apprenticeships|access-date=2016-09-17}}</ref>

Clark has three daughters - Milla, Andi and Billie.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rabbitohs.com.au/team/profiles/jason-clark.html|title=Jason Clark|publisher=Rabbitohs|access-date=2016-09-17}}</ref>

==Records and achievements==
* 2008 South Sydney NYC Players’ Player for the under 20s side award
* 2009 South Sydney NYC Players’ Player for the under 20s side award
* 2009 South Sydney NYC Best and Fairest award
* 2009 round 25 - club debut
* 2013 Selected for NSW Cup team of the year
* 2014 Won NRL Telstra Premiership Grand Final with Souths
* 2015 Won NRL Auckland Nines with Souths
* 2015 Won World Club Challenge with Souths
* 2015 Played for NSW City Origin 
* 2015 Played 100 first grade games (all for South Sydney)
* 2015 Played 150 grade games for a club (all for South Sydney, Including NYC)
* 2015 Awarded South Sydney Burrow Appreciation Award 
* 2016 Awarded South Sydney [[Bob McCarthy]] Clubman of the Year Award
